text.title: MLP Planning Process
text.summary: Five-step process for planning the gap between a validated MVP and a Minimum Lovable Product.

rule.statement: For each core feature, rate 1–5 on all four layers. Any feature below 4 on any layer is a gap that must be closed before declaring MLP.
rule.rationale: The audit makes the gap visible and concrete, replacing "the UX needs work" with specific layer deficits per feature.

rule.statement: Scan four moments for delight: first impression (onboarding), core action (the main thing users do), success moments (when they win), and recovery moments (when things go wrong).
rule.rationale: Delight in error states is disproportionately memorable — it converts frustration into loyalty. Most products ignore this.

list.item: Speed delight: faster than expected (instant search results)
Simplicity delight: easier than expected (one-click checkout)
Anticipation delight: knows what they need (smart defaults)
Celebration delight: acknowledges success (confetti on completion)

rule.statement: Apply the formula: polish priority = user pain × frequency × visibility. Only invest in high-frequency, high-visibility interactions.
rule.rationale: Polish on a rarely-seen screen is invisible to most users. The same effort on the daily core action compounds across every session.

section.title: Step 4: Define MLP Criteria

rule.statement: Write explicit MLP criteria for your target user segment covering all four layers with measurable outcomes. Template: "For [user segment] MLP means: [functional criteria], [reliable criteria], [usable criteria], [delightful criteria]. Measured by: [verification method]."
rule.rationale: Without explicit criteria, MLP becomes subjective and the team will never agree it has been reached.

section.reference: Full MLP plan template including completion criteria section

rule.statement: For each feature, document: current state, MLP target, what is missing, and effort. This becomes the MLP backlog.
rule.rationale: The gap analysis converts the audit rating into actionable tasks with known scope.
